source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive (Remote)
source_jobs.description:
About Rescale

Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t—and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs.

You Will Be Responsible For the Following:

 - Prospecting and driving sales at named and territory accounts in an assigned sales region and attaining quarterly and annual quotas. Our AEs carry a $1.2M-$1.6M http://1.6M quota, depending on level, with uncapped accelerators. 

 - Engage and provide sales consulting to Executives and senior business leaders to influence investment in the Rescale technology. While you will have a 2:1 resource ratio to our pre-sales engineers, we deeply educate our Account Executives so that they can have technical conversations with all customer points of contact. 

 - Develop and coordinate Target Account Plans for major identified opportunities. Although we have a well developed lead generation engine, our AEs create 60% of their own pipeline. 

 - Coordinate activities with other organizations within the company required to execute Account Plans.  Entrepreneurial self-starters flourish here. 

 - Qualify and close new name business from marketing leads, establishing a significant number of new logo customers. Our AEs will drive 75% of their quota in new logo customer wins.

 - Provide sales support to existing customers as needed while driving renewal business, and new incremental business. Our AEs will drive 25% of their quota through expansion of existing accounts.

 - Provide accurate forecasts on a regular basis as well as other information as required. You will forecast weekly your commit, most likely, best case and pipeline numbers to provide transparency of your regional activities.

What Will Make You Successful:

 - Hustle - you are excited about getting out into the field and creating business.

 - Grit - ability to overcome setbacks and persist until you win.

 - Flexibility - ability to adapt to new approaches, new markets, and new technology paradigms. 

 - Entrepreneurship - building your business through creativity and smart application of the meshing of your experience and what you learn about new markets and technologies.

 - Ownership - you take ownership of your accounts, your territory, and your performance.

 - Team Player - you work well with others and encourage all boats to rise together.

Key Qualifications:

 - Bachelor’s degree.

 - Minimum of 7 years in a related sales position with a record of success.

 - CRM system experience (i.e. Salesforce).

 - Advanced problem-solving skills. Ability to build one on one relationships with customers and identify their business needs. 

 - Advanced organizational, interpersonal, and presentation skills.

 - Advanced written and verbal communication skills.

 - Advanced negotiation skills. Naturally persuasive and persistent to achieve goals.

 - General computer skills in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and the G Suite of products.
   

Rescale is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age. As part of our standard hiring process for new employees, employment with Rescale will be contingent upon successful completion of a comprehensive background check. Here at Rescale, we are committed to being transparent in our policies around candidate privacy. For more details on the information Rescale collects in your application, please view the Re

source_jobs.title: Senior Solutions Architect
source_jobs.description:
About Rescale

Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t—and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs.

We are looking to add a Senior Solutions Architect to our team in the US!

As a Solutions Architect, you are responsible for leading and owning technical engagements with our customers. You work closely with prospects, customers, and internal teams in a consultative technical role to help customers accelerate their HPC workloads in the cloud with the Rescale platform. You will play a critical role in the success of Rescale and enjoy the opportunity for personal and career growth. Due to the nature of the industries we work with and customers involved in government-related projects that will be completed in this role, this role will require a US person.

Responsibilities:

 - Lead in coordinating and executing all technical activities throughout the customer pre-sales engagement, such as customer meetings, presentations, demonstrations, and proof of concepts.  Our most successful SA’s lead and complete 2-3 POC’s for strategic customers a quarter.

 - Work independently to analyze technical needs, requirements, the customer’s current infrastructure, operations, and workflows. Our SA’s point of contact is usually a literal rocket scientist, CFD or FEA engineer, or aerospace engineer, where you will learn their technical workflow and help migrate their workflows to the cloud and show them how Rescale can make them run faster.

 - Gain a deep knowledge of customer’s workflows and HPC environments to provide a tailored solution with unique value prop. Our customers sit in the most innovative technologies in the world, from genome sequencing to aeronautical design to crash testing and more.

 - Work with customers to define and execute customers’ digital transformation strategy to migrate workloads from on-prem to the cloud. Our SA’s create strategic visions that span from 1-5 years.

 - Articulate and present Rescale solutions at conferences, user groups, webinars, etc. Our SA’s attend and present at 2-3 regional and national conferences throughout the year. 

 

Key Qualifications:

 - B.S. in engineering, computer science, math, physics or equivalent, M.S. preferred.

 - Obsessed with providing the best experience and solutions to the customers.

 - Enjoy solving difficult problems and strive to find the best solutions.

 - At least 5 years of software, hardware, or cloud experience in a technical role.

 - Experience presenting highly technical topics in an easy-to-understand manner.

 - Periodic travel is required.

 - Due to export control-related projects, must be a US Person.

Preferred Qualifications:

 - 5 years of enterprise cloud, hardware, or software technical experience.

 - Understanding of traditional enterprise sales processes.

 - General knowledge in at least one of the high performance computing (HPC) disciplines (such as CFD, FEA, Molecular Dynamics, Weather Forecasting, Computational Chemistry, Reservoir (Seismic) Simulation, Media Rendering, Machine Learning, Financial etc).

 - Experience with enterprise customers in one or more of the industry verticals we serve including aerospace, automotive, life sciences, oil & gas, semiconductor, or EDA.

 - Experience with at least one of HPC simulation software (such as packages from ANSYS, Siemens, Dassault Systèmes, COMSOL, AVL, Altair, PTC, Cadence, Synopsys, Autodesk, OpenFOAM, LAMMPS. GROMACS, NAMD, etc

source_jobs.title: Director of Accounting
source_jobs.description:
Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t—and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs

We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Director of Accounting to join our team.

Role Overview:

The Director of Accounting is a critical leadership position within the finance department, driving the accuracy and efficiency of our accounting operations. In this role, you will be responsible for leading our accounting operations, ensuring the accuracy and integrity of our financial records, and providing strategic financial insights to drive our business forward. The ideal candidate possesses a strong technical accounting foundation, exceptional communication and leadership skills, and the ability to excel in a fast-paced environment.

Responsibilities:

 - Accounting Operations:
   
   - Oversee and guide daily accounting operations, including general ledger accounting, accounts payable and receivable, and month-end and year-end close processes.
   
   - Ensure compliance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) and all relevant regulations.
   
   - Develop and maintain accounting policies and procedures to ensure effective and efficient internal controls.

 - Financial Reporting:
   
   - Prepare and review monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements for internal and external stakeholders.
   
   - Analyze financial data and provide insights to management on key performance indicators and trends.

 - Revenue Recognition:
   
   - Oversee the revenue recognition process, ensuring compliance with ASC 606 and other relevant guidance.
   
   - Review customer contracts and assess proper revenue recognition treatment.
   
   - Work closely with sales and legal teams to address revenue recognition matters.

 - Audit and Tax Support:
   
   - Oversee and manage the annual financial audit and income tax preparation processes.
   
   - Oversee audit schedules and support internal audits.
   
   - Ensure compliance with SOX requirements.

 - Team Leadership:
   
   - Cultivate a collaborative and high-performing team environment.
   
   - Effectively delegate tasks and monitor team performance.
   
   - Mentor and develop the team, preparing them for future growth within the organization.

 - Process Improvement:
   
   - Identify opportunities to streamline and automate accounting processes.
   
   - Implement process improvements to enhance efficiency and accuracy.
   
   - Stay abreast of best practices in accounting and technology.
   
   - Proactively seek out opportunities to improve and scale financial systems and processes in anticipation of future growth and the transition to the Controller role.

Qualifications:

 - Education: Bachelor's degree in Accounting or related field; CPA preferred.

 - Experience: 10+ years of progressive accounting experience, including experience in a leadership role.

 - Technical Skills: Strong technical accounting knowledge, GAAP expertise, and in-depth understanding of revenue recognition principles (ASC 606). Experience with NetSuite is required.

 - Leadership Skills: Proven ability to lead, inspire, and develop a high-performing team.

 - Communication Skills: Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with stakeholders across the organization.

 - Problem-Solving Skills: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a proactive and solutions-oriented approach

source_jobs.title: Engagement Manager, Defense & National Security
source_jobs.description:
Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional high performance computing (HPC) can’t—and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs.

Building on our longstanding track record of success serving enterprise customers across industries, Rescale has invested significantly in our product to meet the needs of public sector customers, including FedRAMP Moderate and DoD Impact Level 5 (in process) authorizations. We have the privilege of working closely with customers solving some of the most challenging and pressing problems facing our society today. Join us in ramping our efforts to bring Rescale’s capabilities to these customers as they drive mission impact.

We are seeking a Public Sector Engagement Manager to lead our engagements in defense and national security! You will be an integral member of the Rescale Public Sector team, a high-growth business unit within the company. Come build with us!

In this role, you will be responsible for:

 - Developing and growing relationships within the U.S. government, notably the Department of Defense, and the defense industrial base.

 - Meaningfully contributing to the growth of Rescale Public Sector by driving sales to meet or exceed quarterly and annual revenue quotas for your portfolio (both renewals and new business). Our AEs carry an approximately $2-3M quota, depending on level, with uncapped accelerators.

 - Prospecting for and qualifying new opportunities and leading capture activities within assigned territory; building robust short term and long term pipeline; closing major new accounts.

 - Managing complex accounts by developing an expert understanding of customer needs and use cases, and creating and executing strategic account plans.

 - Leveraging Systems Integrator, Reseller, and key technology partner relationships for co-selling, co-solutioning, and market expansion activities.

 - Engaging with cross-functional partners within the company to deliver an excellent customer experience.

 - Regularly highlighting customer developments and providing accurate, high-fidelity forecasts to guide the growth of Rescale Public Sector.

Key Qualifications:

 - 5+ years of proven success selling novel and complex software or technology solutions to the U.S. government, notably the Department of Defense, and defense industrial base.

 - Demonstrated excellence in strategic, enterprise-level account management.

 - Experience growing a greenfield sales territory at a high-growth company.

 - Expert-level command of federal contracting practices, compliance requirements, (e.g, FedRAMP, DoD ILs), and the Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution (PPBE) process.

 - Exceptional interpersonal skills. Ability to build strong, high-trust relationships with customers and understand their needs.

 - Demonstrated ability and drive to independently source and close opportunities in the Public Sector.

 - Experience expanding market reach through engagement with systems integrators, resellers, and distributors.

 - Advanced written and verbal communication, presentation, and negotiation skills. Naturally persuasive and persistent to achieve ambitious goals.

 - Must be a U.S. citizen with the ability to obtain and maintain a U.S. Security Clearance (TS/SCI).

 - Willingness to travel frequently to meet customers and prospects on-site, at industry conferences, and events.



Rescale is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from a

source_jobs.title: Senior Solutions Architect - Data & AI
source_jobs.description:
Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t—and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs.

We are seeking an experienced Solutions Architect to join our team.

About the Forge Team

The Forge Team is Rescale’s innovation engine, a fast-moving, cross-functional group building our next-generation products: Rescale Data Intelligence and Rescale AI Physics. We partner closely with customers, technical fellows, and product teams to validate ideas quickly, fail fast, and iterate rapidly. Our mission is to deliver transformative solutions that enable engineering organizations to harness data intelligence, AI Physics models, and emerging agentic engineering workflows to accelerate innovation.




ROLE SUMMARY

We are seeking a Solutions Architect to join the Strategic Solutions / GTM team within Forge. In this role, you’ll work on the cutting edge of Data Intelligence and AI Physics, engaging with strategic customers to understand their engineering workflows, shape adoption strategies, and influence product direction.

This is a highly collaborative, customer-facing role where you will bridge customers, product teams, and field teams, helping validate hypotheses, accelerate adoption, and deliver value from next-generation, AI-driven engineering workflows, including agentic engineering and AI Physics simulation models.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Partner with account teams to engage with strategic customers, uncovering workflows and technical needs across simulation, data, and AI.

 - Design end-to-end solutions leveraging Rescale Data Intelligence and Rescale AI Physics to enable next-generation engineering workflows.

 - Set the strategic vision for engagement by aligning Rescale’s Data Intelligence and AI Physics capabilities with customer priorities in simulation, AI, and digital engineering.

 - Build trusted relationships with technical leaders and stakeholders, from engineers to technical fellows, to influence adoption strategies and long-term technology roadmaps.

 - Capture and document customer requirements, workflow gaps, and adoption blockers.

 - Provide structured field feedback to Product and Engineering to guide roadmap prioritization and the evolution of agentic engineering capabilities.

 - Collaborate on iterative testing cycles to validate new features and capabilities before GA release.

 - Develop technical collateral, including solution briefs, integration guides, and best practices for AI Physics and agentic engineering workflows.

 - Support internal enablement by contributing to training materials and field readiness resources for Data & AI.

 - Work with Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success to accelerate adoption and drive measurable impact of Data Intelligence and AI Physics solutions.

 - Operate as a strategic overlay on top of Rescale’s core team, providing Data & AI guidance to elevate customer engagements, define strategic implementation plans, and ensure successful adoption.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience
   
   - 3–6 years in solutions architecture, customer success, technical consulting, or product-facing engineering roles.
   
   - Track record of working effectively in fast-moving, experimental environments, where iteration and adaptability are key.

 - Technical & Strategic Expertise
   
   - Familiarity with simulation workflows, engineering data management, or AI/ML integration.
   
   - Understanding of AI Physics models, agentic engi

source_jobs.title: Senior Partner Sales Manager, CSPs
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RESCALE

Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t, and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs.

We are seeking a Senior Partner Sales Manager, CSPs to join our team!




ABOUT THE ROLE

Rescale is hiring a Senior Partner Sales Manager, CSPs, to help scale pipeline and revenue through our Cloud Service Provider ecosystem. This is a field-oriented partner sales role focused on turning CSP relationships into measurable co-sell outcomes. You will work directly with partner sellers, solution architects, and account teams to identify, shape, and advance joint opportunities.

This is not a passive relationship management role. We are looking for a builder and operator who can activate partner field teams, create momentum inside complex accounts, and convert partner activity into qualified pipeline. You should be comfortable operating with urgency, bringing structure to ambiguity, and carrying accountability for partner-sourced business results.


WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Drive partner-sourced pipeline generation through strategic CSPs, with a strong focus on field-level co-sell execution and top-of-funnel creation

 - Build trusted working relationships with CSP account managers, partner sellers, specialists, and solution architects to uncover and develop joint opportunities

 - Translate Rescale’s CSP go-to-market strategy into repeatable field execution across target accounts, territories, and motions

 - Identify, qualify, and progress partner-referred opportunities in close alignment with Rescale account executives and sales leadership

 - Lead account mapping, opportunity planning, and joint follow-up with CSP teams to create durable co-sell engagement rather than one-off introductions

 - Enable partner field teams on Rescale’s value proposition, differentiated use cases, and the business outcomes we drive for engineering and R&D customers

 - Execute operational requirements tied to co-sell success, including deal registration, funding motions, marketplace coordination, pipeline hygiene, and forecast accuracy

 - Provide clear feedback from the field to improve partner messaging, plays, enablement content, and GTM programs over time


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - You consistently generate qualified partner-referred pipeline and help convert partner activity into real sales momentum

 - CSP sellers know who you are, trust your judgment, and see Rescale as a credible solution for their customers

 - You move quickly, follow through relentlessly, and create momentum across multiple accounts without losing rigor

 - You help Rescale sellers and CSP teams stay aligned on where to focus, how to engage, and what it will take to win

 - You bring discipline to the operating cadence of the role, from partner outreach and opportunity qualification to pipeline inspection and execution follow-through

 - You combine strong field instincts with sound judgment, knowing when to push, when to qualify out, and where to invest time for the highest return


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 7+ years of experience in partner sales, partner development, business development, enterprise sales, or related go-to-market roles in cloud, SaaS, or enterprise technology

 - Meaningful experience working with Cloud Service Providers such as AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud

 - Proven ability to generate pipeline through partners, not just manage relationships around existing business

 - Strong u

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager, Accounting
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RESCALE

Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t—and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs

We are seeking a highly motivated Senior Accounting Manager to join our team!

As the Senior Accounting Manager, you will play a key role in optimizing accounting operations, supporting scalability, and enabling impactful change at a fast growing company. You will operate as a highly hands-on individual contributor, collaborating closely with cross-functional partners across Finance, Tax, Payroll, and Operations.

In This Role, You Will Be Responsible For:

 - Leading the month-end close across U.S. and international entities, ensuring timely and accurate accounting for operating expenses, banking activity, accruals, prepaids, fixed assets, and key balance sheet accounts.

 - Preparing and reviewing balance sheet reconciliations and flux analyses, ensuring accounts are fully substantiated with third-party support, clear documentation, and timely resolution of reconciling items.

 - Supporting revenue accounting processes to ensure accurate application of ASC 606, partnering with leadership on complex or non-standard arrangements.

 - Ensuring accuracy and integrity of payroll accounting across U.S. and international entities, including review of payroll journals, reconciliations, and related tax and liability accounts to ensure completeness and compliance.

 - Driving operational accuracy and efficiency across AP and AR, ensuring timeliness and alignment with internal controls and company policies.

 - Owning the execution of U.S. sales and indirect tax compliance, including monitoring nexus, managing registrations, coordinating with Avalara and external advisors, and reviewing filings for accuracy and completeness.

 - Preparing and reviewing audit schedules, PBC requests, and supporting workpapers for external audits and regulatory reviews.

 - Leading process, systems, and close-efficiency improvements, including standardizing month-end procedures, enhancing reconciliation workflows, and identifying automation opportunities.
   

Key Qualifications:

 - 5-7+ years of progressive accounting experience, ideally combining public accounting and industry experience, with exposure to high-growth technology, SaaS, and/or pre-IPO environments.

 - Big 4 or large public accounting firm experience.

 - CPA strongly preferred.

 - Strong technical foundation in U.S. GAAP with hands-on experience applying accounting principles in a dynamic business setting.

 - Experience operating in fast-scaling environments, with comfort navigating ambiguity and shifting priorities.

 - Proficiency with NetSuite strongly preferred; experience with close automation, spend management, payroll, or billing systems (e.g., FloQast, Airbase, BambooHR, or similar) is a plus.

 - Demonstrated ability to improve, standardize, and automate accounting processes to drive scalability, efficiency, and strong internal controls.

 - High standards for accuracy and documentation, with attention to detail while maintaining a broader business perspective.

 - Analytical mindset with the ability to manage competing priorities in a high-growth setting.

 - Clear and confident communicator capable of collaborating cross-functionally on accounting execution matters.

 - Collaborative, team-oriented professional who operates independently and drives outcomes.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t—and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs.

We are seeking a systems-oriented Software Engineer, Backend engineer to join our team!

Some of the things you will work on and impact:

 - own end-to-end delivery of essential, core functionality for Rescale customers as well as rapidly iterating on new product ideas.

 - work with your teammates to build and maintain high quality automation for building and testing our main customer-facing platform.

 - develop and maintain critical areas of the product including identity management, elastic cloud workstations, and workflow automation.

What we’re looking for:

 - 2+ years of experience building and operating performant, non-trivial backend systems in production.

 - Expertise or strong interest in the following areas: web performance optimization (including ORM and DB profiling), deploying and scaling web applications using cloud services, application security

 - High enthusiasm for supporting both rapid proof-of-concept prototypes as well as robust, mission critical components for both internal and external stakeholders.

 - Comfortable with ambiguity, strong user-first mindset, and ability to come up with non-regrettable technical solutions.

 - Flexibility and dedication to work in a team and ship exceptional software.

 - BS in Computer Science or equivalent experience.

Diversity, equity, inclusion and belonging are fundamental to our success. We strive to be a workplace where we:

 - Welcome ALL and ALL are welcome.

 - Show up with our whole selves or whichever parts of ourselves we’re comfortable sharing, and be ready to see the same in others.

 - Be ready to learn, unlearn, and learn again.

 - Respect others and their opinions. We practice empathy in the way we interact with each other and celebrate uniqueness as a team.

 - Seek to understand - ask more questions than one makes statements.

 - Provide safety from judgment and preserve a collaborative environment.

Rescale is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age. As part of our standard hiring process for new employees, employment with Rescale will be contingent upon successful completion of a comprehensive background check. Here at Rescale, we are committed to being transparent in our policies around candidate privacy. For more details on the information Rescale collects in your application, please view the Rescale Applicant Privacy Policy https://rescale.com/company/legal/applicant-privacy-notice/ here.

source_jobs.title: Application Engineer, AI Physics
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RESCALE

Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t—and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs

We are seeking a highly motivated Application Engineer, AI Physics to help build and deliver the next generation of AI Physics capabilities at Rescale!

This is a hands-on, builder-oriented role that sits at the intersection of product development, applied AI, and customer engineering workflows.

You will work directly with the Rescale AI/ML team to build and ship AI Physics capabilities, while also engaging with customers to apply those capabilities to real simulation data and workflows. The role is customer-facing, but product-first: your work with customers is expected to feed directly back into reusable product features and capabilities.

This role is ideal for engineers who enjoy working deep in the codebase, pressure-testing new technology with real users, and turning early prototypes into scalable product capabilities.



In This Role, You Will Be Responsible For:

 - Build and ship AI Physics capabilities alongside the Rescale AI/ML team

 - Turn real customer simulation data into AI-ready pipelines using the core AI Physics product

 - Execute and harden early AI Physics POCs so they are repeatable, reusable, and production-ready

 - Debug and resolve issues across data preprocessing, models, inference, and workflow orchestration

 - Work directly with customer engineers to understand their workflows and technical constraints

 - Close gaps between customer workflows and product capabilities, feeding concrete learnings back into the product roadmap

 - Act as a builder first, with the ability to be forward-deployed on critical customer engagements when needed

Key Qualifications:

 - Strong software engineering fundamentals, with heavy emphasis on Python

 - Hands-on experience with ML or data-driven systems in production contexts

 - Familiarity with simulation workflows and engineering data

 - Experience building and modifying data pipelines and ML workflows

 - Ability to debug complex issues across data, models, and workflow orchestration

 - Comfortable working directly with customer engineers on technical problems
   

Customer Engagement & Travel

 - This role includes direct customer engagement, particularly during POCs and early deployments

 - Expect occasional travel to customer sites for deep technical working sessions, workshops, or critical milestones

 - Travel is purposeful and tied to execution, not routine sales support
   
   

Why This Role Matters

AI Physics Application Engineers play a critical role in ensuring that AI Physics capabilities are not only technically sound, but also usable, scalable, and trusted by engineering teams. Your work will directly influence product direction, customer adoption, and the success of AI Physics deployments in production.


Rescale is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age. As part of our standard hiring process for new employees, employment with Rescale will be contingent upon successful completion of a comprehensive background check. Here at Rescale, we are committed to being transparent in our policies around candidate privacy. For more details on the information Rescale collects in your application, please view the Rescale Applicant Privacy Policy https://rescale.com

source_jobs.title: Senior HPC Engineer, Services
source_jobs.description:
Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t—and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs

We are seeking an HPC Engineer to join our team!

At Rescale, this means you will work on and impact:

 - Become an expert in the Rescale platform and its application to customer workflows.

 - Deliver deep technological solutions and support related to some of Rescale’s most powerful features.

 - Publish, manage and optimize complex HPC software for our most demanding customers.

 - Develop and implement processes and best practices associated with HPC services and support.

 - Advocate customer needs internally and working collaboratively with the sales, product and engineering teams to achieve customer success goals and contribute to overall company objectives.

 - Actively work with the customer support team to ensure customer value realization is improved through every interaction to drive increased customer satisfaction and retention.

 - Identify opportunities for increased productivity through automation and tools and implement these solutions.

 - Design and improve processes to drive customer satisfaction by consistently meeting or exceeding commitments to customers and improving quality of deliverables.

 - Provide technical support to our customers, independent software vendors and strategic partners.

 - Solve various problems related to the software customers are running on Rescale, requiring a high level of knowledge of the software and an in-depth knowledge of the environment the software runs in.

 - Maintain the software available on Rescale, including installing and benchmarking the software, and optimizing the software’s performance for the environment it runs in.



What we’re looking for:

 - 5+ years experience with HPC Systems and HPC Applications
   
   - Experience with Linux/Unix and Windows operating system environments
   
   - Experience with scripting languages like Bash and Python
   
   - Familiarity with cloud computing platforms such as AWS, Azure or Google Cloud
   
   - High-level knowledge of HPC, schedulers, MPI, containers, kubernetes, and GPU computing
   
   - Familiarity with scientific computing libraries and tools like OpenMP and CUDA

 - Experience applying AI-assisted workflows as part of the software development lifecycle — using AI agents, code generation, and automation as multipliers for engineering velocity and quality

 - General knowledge in at least one CAE discipline (CFD, FEA, EDA, ML/AI, Molecular Dynamics, Weather Forecasting, Optics, etc.)

 - Experience working with diverse stakeholders across IT, engineering, and R&D

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills to interact with colleagues and cross-functional teams and third parties

 - BS/BA degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or related field

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineering Manager, Core Cloud Compute - AI
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RESCALE

Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t—and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs

We are seeking a highly motivated Senior Software Engineering Manager to lead the engineering team that owns Rescale's core cloud computing orchestration engine. Their systems drive a large portion of daily customer platform usage, running jobs across multiple public clouds globally.

At Rescale, this means you will work on and impact:

 - Developing technical excellence and leadership among the members of a high-performing engineering team.

 - Fostering effective use of AI-assisted software development across your team — from code agents and AI-augmented code review to automated operational tooling — and contribute to org-wide standards for how we integrate AI into the SDLC.

 - Scaling and extending Rescale's core job orchestration engine, leveraging VM and Kubernetes-based compute for large scale engineering simulations, across multiple public cloud providers.

 - Maintaining high operational expectations on core product — driving incident response, SLO discipline, and reduction of toil and incident load.

 - Driving alignment across EPD (Engineering, Product and Design) and with customer-facing teams (Support, Solutions, Sales) on product delivery and technical architecture.

What we're looking for:

 - Track record of leveling up engineers and building collaboration within the team.

 - Evidence of strong end-to-end ownership of their own and their team's decisions and product charter, including the operational posture of a 24/7 production system.

 - Ability to credibly engage in architecture discussions on scalable distributed systems — comfortable going head-to-head with senior and staff engineers on design trade-offs.

 - Excited by AI-assisted development and pushes the boundaries of what's possible — using AI agents, code generation, and automation as multipliers for engineering velocity and quality.

 - Clear written and interactive communication.

 - Deep collaboration with Product and ownership in product delivery.

 - Effectively balances the needs of their team (and themselves) with that of the business, including reliability investment vs. product delivery.

What we'll need from you:

 - 3+ years experience managing high-performing Software Engineering teams of 3–10 people, including senior and staff ICs.

 - Technically proficient as an IC, at least to Senior Engineer level, with demonstrated depth in scalable distributed systems.

 - Hands-on experience using AI tools in the SDLC as well as driving AI adoption on a team previously in a sustainable manner.

 - Production experience with at least one major cloud (AWS or Azure) at scale and familiarity with managed Kubernetes (EKS, AKS).

 - Led the delivery of, at least one multi-quarter architectural change with quantifiable impact in the past.

 - Experience managing a SaaS system powering a complex backend product, with meaningful on-call ownership, incident command, and SLO discipline.

Rescale is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age. As part of our standard hiring process for new employees, employment with Rescale will be contingent upon successful completion of a comprehensive background check. Here at Rescale, we are committed to being transparent in our policie

source_jobs.title: Applications Engineers, Agentic Workflows
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RESCALE

Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t—and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs

We are seeking a highly motivated Application Engineer, Agentic Workflows to help build and deliver the next generation of AI Physics capabilities at Rescale!

You will work directly with platform and AI teams to design and ship agent-based capabilities that operate on real engineering tools and data. You will also work closely with customers to apply these agents to real workflows, ensuring what gets built is reliable, repeatable, and productizable.

The role is customer-facing, but product-first. Work done in customer environments must translate into reusable product capabilities rather than one-off solutions.

This role is ideal for engineers who enjoy building real systems, integrating with complex engineering software, and turning early agent prototypes into durable platform capabilities.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build and ship agent-driven automation across engineering and CAE workflows

 - Design and implement agents that interact with engineering tools, simulation data, and workflows

 - Encode engineering workflows as structured, agent-executable steps with clear inputs, outputs, and guardrails

 - Integrate agents into end-to-end pipelines as reusable capabilities rather than one-off scripts

 - Harden early agent use cases into deterministic, production-ready systems

 - Debug and resolve issues across agent logic, tool execution, data handling, and orchestration

 - Work directly with customer engineers to understand real workflows and technical constraints

 - Close gaps between real workflows and platform capabilities, feeding concrete learnings back into product direction

 - Operate as a builder first, with the ability to be forward-deployed on critical engagements when needed


CORE SKILL SET

 - Strong software engineering fundamentals, with heavy emphasis on Python

 - Hands-on experience building automation around engineering or technical software

 - Familiarity with CAE workflows, simulation data, and solver-driven processes

 - Experience building or deploying agent-based or LLM-driven systems

 - Experience orchestrating tools, scripts, or workflows across multiple systems

 - Ability to design deterministic, reliable automation around complex tools

 - Experience turning internal automation into reusable, product-grade components

 - Comfortable debugging across tool execution, data handling, and orchestration layers


CUSTOMER ENGAGEMENT & TRAVEL

This role includes direct customer engagement during agent deployments and early workflow rollouts. Expect occasional travel to customer sites for deep technical working sessions or critical milestones. Travel is execution-focused and tied to building and deploying real capabilities.




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

AI Agent Applications Engineers ensure that agent-based automation is not just experimental, but reliable, usable, and embedded into real engineering workflows.

Your work will directly influence how engineering organizations adopt agent-driven automation, and will shape the core capabilities that become part of the platform.



Rescale is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age. As part of our standard hiring process for new employees, employment with Rescale will b

source_jobs.title: Solutions Architect (Japan)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RESCALE

Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t—and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs 

We are looking to add a Solutions Architect to our team in Japan! The Solutions Architect is responsible to lead and own the technical engagement. This position involves working closely with prospects, customers and internal teams in a consultative technical role to help customers implement HPC workloads in the cloud with the ScaleX platform. The ability to research problems and find solutions to a wide variety of problems is paramount.  This role plays a critical part in the future success of Rescale in APAC and presents a high opportunity for personal and career growth.


In this role, you will be responsible for the following:

 - Lead in coordinating and executing all technical activities throughout the customer engagements, such as customer meetings, presentations, demonstrations, and proof of concept.

 - Work independently to analyze technical needs, requirements, the customer's’ current infrastructure, operations, and engineering workflows.

 - Gain deep knowledge of customer and ISV’s workflows and IT environments to provide a tailored solution with unique value prop.

 - Work with customers to define and execute customers’ digital transformation strategy to migrate workloads from on-premises to the cloud.

 - Contribute to Statement of Work and responses for RFI / RFP.

 - Responsible for articulating and presenting Rescale solutions at conferences, user groups, webinars, etc.

 - Collaborate with Product Management to improve Rescale products and the customers’ experience using these products.

Key qualifications include:

 - B.S. or M.S. in engineering, computer science, or equivalent preferred.

 - 3+ years of enterprise cloud, hardware, or software technical experience.

 - Deep understanding of both traditional enterprise sales process and SaaS sales models.

 - Expertise and/or interest in the following technical areas: high-performance computing (HPC), computer-aided engineering (CAE), IaaS architecture, enterprise security, and networking.

 - Experience with enterprise customers in one or more of the industry verticals we serve including aerospace, automotive, life sciences, oil & gas, and semiconductors.

 - Ability to manage multiple projects which are complex in nature and coordinate colleagues across diverse teams and locations.

 - Demonstrate understanding of HPC, Scheduler, IaaS, scripting languages and how these tools are used and deployed by our customers.

 - Flexibility and dedication to delivering value for customers.

 - Fluent in Japanese language is a must.

 - Fluent in English.

About your team at Rescale and who you will work with:

 - Manager: Hitoshi Sato https://www.linkedin.com/in/htsst/, Technical Director Japan at Rescale.

 - You will work with:  Support, Engineering, HPC Engineering, Product, and Sales.

Rescale is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age. As part of our standard hiring process for new employees, employment with Rescale will be contingent upon successful completion of a comprehensive background check.  




#LI-Remote

source_jobs.title: Technical Support Engineer - Korea
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RESCALE

Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t, and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs.

We are seeking a Technical Support Engineer to join our Korea team!

In this role, you will be responsible for the following:

 - Becoming an expert in the Rescale platform

 - Learning and supporting the state-of-the-art HPC workflows on the cloud

 - Providing the highest quality technical support to our customers

 - Working in a growing globally distributed support team

 - Operating in an interdisciplinary team environment with developers, engineers, business, and sales personnel

 - Rotating On-Call responsibilities as needed

Key Qualifications include:

 - BA/BS or higher in Engineering, Computer Science or related field

 - Experience using Linux shell, preferably as a system administrator

 - Deep understanding of basic compute technology (storage, memory, CPU, GPU, IO)

 - Understanding and familiarity with HPC and parallel computing (parallel scaling, MPI, shared. memory)

 - Knowledge of basic networking concepts (firewalls, ports, throughput, latency) and cloud computing technology

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Nice to Haves: Hands on experience with parallel workflows, engineering simulations, benchmarking, HPC, schedulers, and MPI Experience with shell and Python scripting

 - Experience in working with Enterprise customers

 - Experience in a customer-facing role such as customer support, customer success, pre-sales, etc.

 - Experience and strong interest in AI technology and agentic workflows

 - Fluency in Korean and working knowledge of English

Diversity, equity, inclusion and belonging are fundamental to our success. We strive to be a workplace where we:

 - Welcome ALL and ALL are welcome.

 - Show up with our whole selves or whichever parts of ourselves we’re comfortable sharing, and be ready to see the same in others.

 - Be ready to learn, unlearn, and learn again.

 - Respect others and their opinions. We practice empathy in the way we interact with each other and celebrate uniqueness as a team.

 - Seek to understand - ask more questions than one makes statements.

 - Provide safety from judgment and preserve a collaborative environment.
   
   
   Who you will be working with:
   
   Manager: Mao Shan Lim https://jp.linkedin.com/in/limmaoshan, APAC Support Lead
   
   Team: https://www.linkedin.com/in/misha-e-dennisAlisa Matsui https://www.linkedin.com/in/alisa-matsui-29b057169/ (Japan Support Team), Junghoon Kim https://www.linkedin.com/in/junghoon-john-kim-8a825a154/ (Korea Customer Success Team)

Rescale is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age. As part of our standard hiring process for new employees, employment with Rescale will be contingent upon successful completion of a comprehensive background check. Here at Rescale, we are committed to being transparent in our policies around candidate privacy. source_jobs.title: Technical Accounting Manager
source_jobs.description:
Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we are transforming how products are developed through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world's largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission driven team that is unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing.

We are seeking a highly motivated Technical Accounting Manager to join our team.

In this role, you will serve as a hands-on individual contributor with deep ownership of technical accounting matters across the business, with particular strength in ASC 606. This person will be a key partner to Finance, Sales, Legal, Tax, Payroll, and Operations, and will provide support across several important areas, including technical accounting research and guidance, drafting accounting policies and memos, supporting sales tax and income tax work, assisting with audit, tax, and process improvement initiatives from a project management perspective, partnering with the team to identify pain points and address them through AI, workflow improvements, and better processes, and cross training to provide support and process redundancy across accounting operations as they absorb the business and improve productivity.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR

 - Leading technical accounting research and preparing clear, well supported accounting memos on complex or non standard matters under U.S. GAAP, with particular strength in ASC 606, ASC 340-40, ASC 340-50, and related guidance

 - Leading contract reviews to assess revenue recognition conclusions, identify accounting implications early, and ensure appropriate treatment is documented before execution

 - Evaluating customer arrangements for ASC 606 implications, including identification of performance obligations, allocation considerations, timing of revenue recognition, contract modifications, and other judgmental areas

 - Supporting accounting analysis for related topics such as contract cost capitalization, commission expensing, and other deal related accounting conclusions

 - Drafting, maintaining, and updating accounting policies and position papers to support a scalable and well controlled accounting environment

 - Serving as a primary technical resource for the finance team on revenue recognition, policy application, and other complex accounting matters

 - Supporting the external audit process by preparing technical memos, audit schedules, and other documentation needed to support the company's accounting positions

 - Supporting tax related workstreams, including coordination and documentation for sales tax and income tax matters

 - Partnering cross functionally with Legal, Sales, RevOps, and other stakeholders to review deal terms and flag accounting issues before they become downstream execution challenges

 - Identifying gaps, pain points, and control weaknesses in current accounting workflows, and helping address them through better processes, automation, AI enabled tools, and improved documentation

 - Creating redundancy across the team by cross training on key accounting processes and serving as backup support where needed

 - Acting as a reliable, hands on team member who can step into high priority areas during audit cycles, close periods, or bandwidth constraints


WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - 6 to 8+ years of progressive accounting experience, required to include both public accounting and industry experience

 - Background in a major public accounting firm is required

 - Strong technical accounting background with demonstrated experience applying ASC 606 and other U.S. GAAP guidance in practice

 - Proven experience researching accounting issues and drafting high quality technical accounting memos and policy documentation

 - Experience reviewing contracts and tra

source_jobs.title: Head of FP&A
source_jobs.description:
Rescale is pioneering the future of engineering and scientific discovery. As the leader in digital engineering, we’re transforming how products are developed—through intelligent automation, applied AI, data management, and the integration of the world’s largest network of engineering and R&D applications. Joining Rescale means becoming part of a diverse, collaborative, and mission-driven team that’s unlocking faster innovation across industries like aerospace, energy, life sciences, and manufacturing. We’re solving complex challenges that traditional HPC can’t—and we’re seeking passionate, curious minds to help build the next wave of breakthroughs

We are seeking a highly motivated Head of FP&A (financial planning and analysis) to own Rescale's FP&A function end to end. You will partner with executive leadership to translate strategy into financial plans, surface the metrics that drive the business, and bring rigor to capital allocation and operating decisions. This role will shape our financial strategy and have a significant impact on Rescale's growth.

At Rescale, this means you will:

 - Develop and own detailed, data-driven financial models and ad-hoc analytical projects and present the results to senior management to support strategic decision-making

 - Generate dashboard reports that monitor KPI’s to track Rescale’s performance, and communicate trends and findings to management to highlight risks and opportunities

 - Develop and execute timely monthly, quarterly, and annual reports, partnering with the accounting team and the business on the close process, ensuring insights, risks, and opportunities are identified and communicated to all stakeholders

 - Perform variance analyses to ensure fiscal responsibility and accountability; identify trends; recommend actions for continual improvement

 - Assist in preparing QBR, Board and other investor-related materials and presentations

 - Continuously identify opportunities to automate and streamline processes with AI and agentic tools to increase the efficiency and effectiveness of the FP&A function

What we’re looking for:

 - Bachelors in Finance, Accounting, Business, Economics, or a highly analytical field (e.g., Engineering, Math, or Computer Science) or equivalent. MBA or similar degree is preferred, but not required.

 - 5+ years of finance experience or a related analytical field

 - Expert financial modeling skills and extensive experience conducting quantitative financial analysis

 - Exceptional communication skills & the necessary confidence and poise to work effectively with senior decision makers

 - Experience in AI, software, software infrastructure, or cloud computing industries preferred

 - Advanced Excel skills and fluency with AI tools (e.g. Claude, Gemini, OpenAI)

 - Located in the Continental US (we are a remote–first company)

Rescale is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age. As part of our standard hiring process for new employees, employment with Rescale will be contingent upon successful completion of a comprehensive background check. Here at Rescale, we are committed to being transparent in our policies around candidate privacy. For more details on the information Rescale collects in your application, please view the Rescale Applicant Privacy Policy https://rescale.com/company/legal/applicant-privacy-notice/ here.

notes: Rescale is a digital engineering company with a strong focus on AI, data management, and HPC. The job postings suggest a diverse and collaborative team with a mission-driven culture.
